title: esquizo.com v7.5
body: [![](http://farm4.static.flickr.com/3228/3128916740_1d9346d856.jpg?v=0)](http://www.esquizo.com)It’s been over a year since the [last version of esquizo.com](http://www.xarop.com/esquizocom-v7/).  It was supposed to be a temporary version since I was forced to migrate from [Joomla](/category/cms/joomla/), which was giving me a lot of problems. I chose [WordPress](/category/cms/wordpress/) because it was the tool that allowed me to migrate the fastest, and the plan was to build the final version on [Drupal](/tag/drupal/) but since I don’t have much time or resources (it’s a project funded solely by advertising), I’ve decided to stick with WordPress for now, as it’s an easy-to-manage and flexible tool thanks to its plugins. In this case, I started with a 3-column [theme](/tag/theme/) that I modified to meet the required needs. I updated it to the latest version of WordPress, 2.7, and took advantage of the new functionality to nest and reply to specific comments. I’ve also added the [WP-Polls](http://lesterchan.net/portfolio/programming/php/ "Visit the plugin’s site") plugin for conducting polls, and the [Tabbed Widgets](http://wordpress.org/extend/plugins/tabbed-widgets/ "Visit the plugin’s site") plugin, which makes it easy to organize multiple widgets into tabs. What do you think?
